description: Tokio runtime tuning — current_thread vs multi_thread

runtime_tuning_multi_thread_round_trip.note: Multi-thread runtime with 2MB stack — current default

runtime_tuning_current_thread_round_trip.note: Current-thread runtime with 2MB stack — L5 low-latency preset

runtime_tuning_scheduling_yield_multi_th.note: yield_now() on multi_thread — just enqueues to local queue

runtime_tuning_scheduling_yield_current_.note: yield_now() on current_thread — must poll reactor each yield

key_findings.current_thread_is_slower_for_quic: current_thread is 8.2% SLOWER for QUIC round-trip (44.87µs vs 41.48µs). This is because quinn uses background I/O threads, so the main thread must both run application logic AND poll kqueue — causing more context switches.
key_findings.multi_thread_is_better_for_quic: The multi_thread runtime lets the I/O thread handle kqueue while the worker thread runs the application. This is the right choice for QUIC-based transports.
key_findings.yield_now_overhead: yield_now() is 475x faster on multi_thread (24ns vs 11.7µs) because current_thread must poll the reactor on every yield. This doesn't affect the round-trip because the round-trip doesn't yield — it blocks on I/O.
key_findings.stack_size_reduction: Reduced thread stack from 8MB to 2MB — saves memory, improves cache. No stack overflow observed in tests.
key_findings.recommendation: Keep multi_thread as default. The RuntimeConfig is available for users who want current_thread (e.g., for non-QUIC transports or embedded use cases).

configuration_changes.stack_size: Reduced default stack from 8MB to 2MB in RuntimeConfig::default()

implication_for_L8_target: The 42µs → <30µs target cannot be achieved via runtime tuning alone. The multi_thread runtime is already optimal for QUIC. The remaining bottleneck is the QUIC protocol processing and kqueue I/O overhead.
